{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient has blue eyes, blonde hair, and fair skin, which are characteristics of phenylketonuria (PKU). The Guthrie test was positive, confirming the diagnosis. PKU is due to a deficiency of the PAH enzyme, which is responsible for converting phenylalanine to tyrosine. Option A is correct. Option B is incorrect because PKU does not involve tryptophan deficiency or white patches of hair. Option C is correct as phenyl acetate is found in the urine of PKU patients. Option D is correct as mental retardation can occur in untreated PKU.", "answer_choice": "B"}
